critic_raw: Four issues: preprocessing leakage from full-dataset fitting, explicit test-set misuse in Section 6, potential misalignment between proportional and temporal split boundaries, and asymmetric preprocessing between transformer and baseline.
all_issues_raised: Preprocessing transformers fit on entire dataset before splitting (Section 4) — contradicts later statement that numerical scaling is fit on training data only
Test set explicitly used for hyperparameter cross-verification — Section 6 confirms test-set contamination
Temporal split proportions (60/20/20 by patient count) may not correspond to temporal thirds of data if patient volume is non-uniform over time
Inconsistent preprocessing: transformer uses full-dataset normalization (Section 4) while TF-IDF baseline uses training-only fitting (Section 5) — asymmetric preprocessing

round_transcripts.critic: Four issues: preprocessing leakage from full-dataset fitting, explicit test-set misuse in Section 6, potential misalignment between proportional and temporal split boundaries, and asymmetric preprocessing between transformer and baseline.
round_transcripts.defender:
**On Issue 1 (Preprocessing leakage):** Section 4 states two things: (a) preprocessing transformers are fit on the full dataset before splitting, and (b) numerical scaling is fit on training data. This internal contradiction in Section 4 means at least some preprocessing uses full-dataset statistics. The broader statement in the first part of Section 4 is the more general and damaging claim. This issue is confirmed.

**On Issue 2 (Test-set misuse):** Section 6 is explicit: test-set cross-verification is used to 'confirm hyperparameter configuration maintains its performance edge' and guide whether to retrain on combined data. This is unambiguous test-set contamination. Confirmed and material.

**On Issue 3 (Split alignment):** If recent years have more patients (common in EHR datasets as documentation volume grows), 60% by patient count may represent fewer than 60% of months. The temporal cut-points in the description may not align with the proportional split. This is a real but minor concern — the design would need to specify both temporal and proportional boundaries clearly.

**On Issue 4 (Preprocessing asymmetry):** If the transformer uses full-dataset normalization while TF-IDF uses training-only fitting, the two models have different preprocessing standards. This creates an inconsistent comparison: the transformer may benefit from richer normalization statistics. Confirmed as a real concern.

round_transcripts.critic: All four issues confirmed. Issue 2 (test-set misuse) is the most severe. Issue 1 is confirmed preprocessing leakage. Issue 4 creates an asymmetric playing field. Issue 3 is a minor specification gap.
round_transcripts.defender: All four issues stand. Issue 2 is fatal to the test estimate. Issues 1 and 4 represent data leakage and inconsistent preprocessing standards. Issue 3 is a minor specification concern.

all_issues_adjudicated: Preprocessing transformers fit on entire dataset before splitting — internal contradiction in Section 4
Test set explicitly used for hyperparameter cross-verification — unambiguous test-set contamination
Inconsistent preprocessing: transformer uses full-dataset normalization; TF-IDF baseline uses training-only fitting
